response:
Emily gathered 24 blueberries which became the starting point for the group's collection. David collected 15 more than Emily which means he had 39 blueberries in total. His amount was also three times what Sophia had which indicates Sophia gathered 13 blueberries. Together the three of them combined their blueberries to use for making muffins.

***

They pooled all their blueberries together and found they had a total of 76 blueberries. Each batch of muffins required 9 blueberries which meant they could make several batches. The number of batches they could make was calculated by dividing the total blueberries by the number needed per batch. This calculation showed how many batches they could prepare for sale.

***

Each batch was sold for 5 dollars which meant the total money earned depended on the number of batches made. After dividing the total blueberries by the number needed per batch they found out how many batches they could make. Multiplying that number by the price per batch gave them the total amount of money they earned from selling the muffins.

***

The process of collecting and selling blueberries brought them together and created a sense of accomplishment. The amount of money they made was directly tied to the number of blueberries they had and how efficiently they used them. Their efforts reflected a blend of teamwork and resourcefulness. Youth.
